Danville Va Nov 18th 1863
        Dear Father and Mother
                      As I now I have an opertunity I will let you know where I am I was
captured at the battle of Chickamaga the 20th Sept. I am well and hope this may find you the
same. The authoraties here allow prisoners to recieve boxes from home providing no
contraband article is sent, and as there is a good prospect of our having to stay a long time
and I need some things, I would like for you to express me a box, send 1 pair drawers &
under Shirt, 2 pr. socks, 2 Handkerchiefs, 2 Towels, some paper, pens, Envelopes, Stamps &
Ink; thread & kneedles, some of the latest novels in town, Butter, Cheese, Crackers, a good
hunk of meat, biscuits, Onions, Tea, Coffee, Salt, Pepper, Sausage, dried Beef, Shugar, a box of
Mustard Stick in other things that you have handy some Tobacco & Cigars will be exceptable
and send it as soon as possible I will write as often as I can but I have to be as brief as
possible give my respects to all inquiring friends and send the things immediately
                                              Your son,
                                                 Geo. F. Alverson
P.S. Put in 2 or 3 decks of cards and address the box as follows
          By Flag of Truce to
      George F. Alverson Co K, 10 Wisc.
         Prison No. 3, 1st floor Danville Va.
             in care of Brigdr Gen J.H. Winder
                          Richmond Va

a knife and Fork, a comb and a big chunk of Cast Steel soap without fail, and shaving soap a
brush and small looking glass, a small Tin bucket or coffee pot or boath, a Tin can full of
Molasses, a little dried fruit
